system_prompt_preview:
You are a senior media strategist at a data-driven advertising agency. You are reviewing the statistical output of a quantitative media audit conducted on a client's paid media campaign. Your job is to synthesize these findings into a clear, strategic, and specific narrative.

RULES YOU MUST FOLLOW:
response: {"executive_summary": "The Spring 2026 Performance Campaign for Meridian Outdoor Co. has identified $243,576 in recoverable efficiency across five diagnostic signals. The most urgent issue is creative fatigue in Paid Social, where social_v1 CTR has decayed 53% from 2.80% to 1.32% with a half-life of 14.5 days, and YouTube is exhibiting severe diminishing returns with a 2.65x marginal CPA ratio (last-30d $151 vs first-30d $57).", "findings": [{"rank": 1, "title": "Paid Social creative social_v1 is severely fatigued \u2014 rotate immediately", "severity": "critical", "narrative": "Creative social_v1 has experienced a 53% CTR collapse (2.80% \u2192 1.32%) with exponential decay confirmed at R\u00b2=0.844 and \u03bb=0.0479/day (p=0.000002). The decay onset occurred at day 33 and the half-life of 14.5 days means performance will continue deteriorating rapidly. Leaving this creative running costs an estimated $130,146 in lost conversions over 30 days, based on daily impressions \u00d7 CTR gap \u00d7 channel CVR \u00d7 $120 AOV.", "recommendation": "Immediately pause social_v1 and replace with social_v2 or social_v3. Schedule creative rotation every 30 days for paid social to prevent recurrence. The optimal rotation point (80% of initial CTR) was approximately day 46.", "estimated_impact": "$130,146", "confidence": "high"}, {"rank": 2, "title": "Programmatic Display is past frequency saturation \u2014 apply a frequency cap of 7", "severity": "critical", "narrative": "Frequency has drifted from 4.0 to 9.7 impressions/user over 90 days. The piecewise CPA model identifies a breakpoint at frequency 7.1, above which CPA increases 41.5% (Pearson r=0.775 with frequency). The channel is currently operating at frequency 9.7 \u2014 2.6 units past the saturation threshold \u2014 generating avoidable CPA inflation.", "recommendation": "Set a frequency cap of 7 in the DSP immediately. Redirect freed impressions toward prospecting audiences or new user pools to maintain reach without over-serving.", "estimated_impact": "$38,638", "confidence": "high"}, {"rank": 3, "title": "Paid Search has untapped daypart opportunity in Prime and Late Night", "severity": "warning", "narrative": "Kruskal-Wallis test confirms significant CVR variance across dayparts (H=520, p\u22480). Prime (20-23h) and Late Night (23-5h) have 1.38\u00d7 average CVR but receive only 5-7% of impressions combined. Shifting 15% of daily clicks to these dayparts yields an estimated $38,021 in incremental conversions over 30 days.", "recommendation": "Increase Paid Search bid multipliers for Prime (20-23h) and Late Night (23-5h) dayparts by 30-40%. Monitor CPA for 2 weeks and reallocate impression share from underperforming midday and afternoon slots.", "estimated_impact": "$38,021", "confidence": "medium"}, {"rank": 4, "title": "YouTube in diminishing returns \u2014 reallocate 20% of budget to CTV", "severity": "critical", "narrative": "YouTube marginal CPA has increased 2.65x from $57 (first 30d) to $151 (last 30d). CTV, by contrast, shows a improving ratio of 0.91\u00d7 at a late CPA of $73. Shifting 20% of YouTube spend to CTV yields an estimated $28,291 in net additional conversions at equivalent spend.", "recommendation": "Shift 20% of YouTube daily budget to CTV immediately. Set a YouTube frequency cap and focus remaining YouTube spend on top-performing audiences only. Re-evaluate YouTube budget in 30 days after performance stabilizes.", "estimated_impact": "$28,291", "confidence": "high"}, {"rank": 5, "title": "Search spend spikes cannibalize Display CPA via audience overlap", "severity": "warning", "narrative": "Paid Search spend increases >15% WoW correlate with Programmatic Display CPA increases of 8-18% in the same period (Pearson r=0.646, p=0.023). When Search scales aggressively, attribution shifts conversions away from Display, artificially inflating Display CPA. Granger causality is not significant at lag=1 (p=0.749), suggesting contemporaneous audience overlap rather than delayed attribution shift.", "recommendation": "Implement audience exclusion lists between Paid Search and Programmatic Display to reduce overlap. Avoid simultaneous large Search budget increases and Display flights. Estimated monthly cannibalization cost is $8,781.", "estimated_impact": "$8,781", "confidence": "medium"}], "cross_channel_insights": "Three of the five critical issues interact: YouTube diminishing returns and Search cannibalization of Display are compounding the audience fatigue problem \u2014 over-served users see both Search and Display, eroding Display efficiency while YouTube spend chases the same diminishing pool of converters. Fixing the frequency cap on Display (breakpoint 7.1) and the YouTube reallocation to CTV will also reduce the cannibalization signal. The creative fatigue fix in Paid Social is independent and should be actioned immediately.", "action_plan": [{"priority": 1, "action": "Pause social_v1 creative in Paid Social and replace with social_v2 or social_v3", "timeline": "immediate", "owner": "media_buyer"}, {"priority": 2, "action": "Set frequency cap of 7 on Programmatic Display in the DSP", "timeline": "immediate", "owner": "media_buyer"}, {"priority": 3, "action": "Shift 20% of YouTube daily budget to CTV and set YouTube frequency cap", "timeline": "this_week", "owner": "media_buyer"}, {"priority": 4, "action": "Increase Paid Search bid multipliers for Prime and Late Night dayparts by 30-40%", "timeline": "this_week", "owner": "media_buyer"}, {"priority": 5, "action": "Implement audience exclusion lists between Paid Search and Programmatic Display to reduce cannibalization; brief creative team on 30-day rotation schedule for Paid Social", "timeline": "this_month", "owner": "analyst"}]}
